name: Fibonacci / Pairing Heap
def: A priority queue with O(1) amortised insert and decrease-key, lazily restructured to defer balancing work.
problem: Cut the decrease-key cost that dominates Dijkstra and Prim on dense graphs, where a binary heap pays O(log n) per relaxation.
mechanism: Maintains a forest of heap-ordered trees; insert and decrease-key just splice or cut nodes and mark parents, deferring consolidation until extract-min, which pays the amortised O(log n).
benefits: O(1) amortised insert, merge and decrease-key
Improves Dijkstra/Prim to O(E + V log V)
limits: Large constant factors and pointer overhead
Poor cache locality — often slower in practice
failures: Real-world slower than a binary heap despite better bounds
Amortised — individual operations can still spike
whenNot: Most workloads (a binary or pairing heap is simpler and faster)
myths: "Fibonacci heaps speed up Dijkstra in practice" — the constants usually lose to a binary heap; the pairing heap is the practical compromise
